BarberProfile.title: BarberProfile
BarberProfile.description: Represents the profile and general settings for the barber using the application. This entity manages the barber's information and overall barbershop settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BarberProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the barber.

email.description: The email address of the barber, used for contact and potentially notifications.

phone.description: The phone number of the barber.

businessName.description: The name of the barbershop business.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the barber profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the barber profile was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Stores information about a barbershop client, including contact details, birth date for age verification, and any specific notes or preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

barberProfileId.description: Reference to the BarberProfile. (Relationship: BarberProfile 1:N Client)

name.description: The full name of the client.

email.description: The email address of the client, used for communication.

phone.description: The phone number of the client.

birthDate.description: The birth date of the client, used to verify age for targeted clientele.

notes.description: Any special notes or preferences for this client, such as preferred style or allergies.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines a service offered by the barbershop, including its name, description, price, and duration. This allows the barber to manage their service catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

barberProfileId.description: Reference to the BarberProfile that offers this service. (Relationship: BarberProfile 1:N Service)

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Corte Masculino', 'Barba', 'Química').

description.description: A detailed description of the service.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the service in minutes.

isActive.description: Indicates if the service is currently active and available for booking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the service was last updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a specific scheduled booking for a client with a barber and service, capturing all details relevant to the appointment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who booked the appointment. (Relationship: Client 1:N Appointment)

serviceId.description: Reference to the Service being provided in this appointment. (Relationship: Service 1:N Appointment)

barberProfileId.description: Reference to the BarberProfile for whom this appointment is scheduled. (Relationship: BarberProfile 1:N Appointment)

startTime.description: The scheduled start date and time of the appointment.

endTime.description: The scheduled end date and time of the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Canceled', 'Rescheduled').

clientNotes.description: Notes provided by the client regarding this specific appointment.

barberNotes.description: Internal notes added by the barber for this specific appointment.

priceAtAppointment.description: The price of the service recorded at the time of the appointment, to account for potential future price changes.

isPaid.description: Indicates whether the payment for this appointment has been received.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment was last updated.

AvailabilityBlock.title: AvailabilityBlock
AvailabilityBlock.description: Represents a specific time block during which the barber is explicitly unavailable for appointments, such as for breaks, personal commitments, or days off.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AvailabilityBlock entity.

barberProfileId.description: Reference to the BarberProfile to whom this availability block applies. (Relationship: BarberProfile 1:N AvailabilityBlock)

startTime.description: The start date and time of the blocked period.

endTime.description: The end date and time of the blocked period.

reason.description: The reason for the unavailability (e.g., 'Lunch Break', 'Day Off', 'Meeting').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the availability block was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the availability block was last updated.

definition.description: Stores the profile and general settings for a specific barber. The `barberProfileId` document ID must match the authenticated user's UID (request.auth.uid) for direct ownership and access.

params.name: barberProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the BarberProfile, which is the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the barber.

definition.description: Stores information about a client belonging to a specific barbershop. Each client document includes the `barberProfileId` for authorization independence, allowing direct ownership checks.

params.name: barberProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the BarberProfile that owns this client record.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Defines a service offered by a specific barbershop. Each service document includes the `barberProfileId` for authorization independence, enabling direct ownership checks without parent `get()` calls.

params.name: barberProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the BarberProfile that offers this service.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Represents a scheduled booking for a client with a barber. Each appointment document includes the `barberProfileId` for authorization independence, ensuring that the barber owns and can manage their appointments directly.

params.name: barberProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the BarberProfile for whom this appointment is scheduled.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Represents a specific time block during which a barber is unavailable. Each availability block document includes the `barberProfileId` for authorization independence, allowing the barber to manage their own schedule blocks.

params.name: barberProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the BarberProfile to whom this availability block applies.

params.name: availabilityBlockId
params.description: The unique identifier for the availability block.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to strictly adhere to the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and simplified Access Modeling, making security rules robust, clear, and debuggable. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** For all subcollections (`clients`, `services`, `appointments`, `availabilityBlocks`), the `barberProfileId` field is denormalized and stored directly within each document. This is explicitly mandated by the schemas provided. This denormalization is crucial as it eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check the parent `BarberProfile` document for ownership. Instead, rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.barberProfileId`, allowing for atomic document operations (e.g., creating a client and an appointment simultaneously within a batch/transaction) without breaking security checks. This design significantly enhances performance and reduces the risk of race conditions or complex rule evaluation failures.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Structural Segregation:** The entire data model is built around a clear hierarchical path structure: `/barberProfiles/{barberProfileId}/<subcollection>/<documentId>`. This structure directly supports secure `list` operations (Query Against Permitted Subset - QAPs). By segregating all data related to a specific barber under their unique `barberProfileId` (which directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`), security rules can enforce that a user can only query or listen to data within their own `/barberProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/...` path. For example, when a barber requests a list of their clients from `/barberProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/clients`, the rule `allow list: if request.auth.uid == barberProfileId;` ensures that the user can only see documents where the `barberProfileId` in the path matches their authenticated UID. This prevents rules from attempting to filter results after the query, which is an anti-pattern. Each collection and subcollection (`barberProfiles`, `clients`, `services`, `appointments`, `availabilityBlocks`) maintains a homogeneous security posture: only the owner (`request.auth.uid` matching `barberProfileId`) has read/write access to their respective documents. This radical consistency drastically simplifies rule writing and debugging.
